name: Airtable
shortDescription: A comprehensive integration that enables seamless interaction with Airtable databases. This extension provides full CRUD operations for bases, tables, records, and comments, along with real-time monitoring capabilities for automated workflows and data synchronization.

actions.name: Get All Bases
actions.description: Retrieves all accessible Airtable bases for the authenticated user. Perfect for AI agents to discover available workspaces and their access permissions before performing data operations.

actions.name: Get Base Schema
actions.description: Retrieves the complete schema for an Airtable base, including all tables, fields, views, and their configurations. Essential for AI agents to understand data structure before performing operations.

actions.name: Create Base
actions.description: Creates a new Airtable base with specified tables and initial structure. Perfect for AI agents to dynamically provision new workspaces and data structures for projects or applications.

actions.name: Create Table
actions.description: Creates a new table within an existing Airtable base with specified fields and structure. Ideal for AI agents to dynamically provision data storage as needed for different use cases.

actions.name: Update Table
actions.description: Updates the name and/or description of an existing table. Useful for AI agents to maintain organized data structures as requirements evolve.

actions.name: Find Record
actions.description: Retrieves a single record from an Airtable table using flexible search options. Perfect for AI agents to locate specific records based on field values or complex search formulas.

actions.name: Find Many Records
actions.description: Retrieves multiple records from an Airtable table using flexible search criteria and formulas. Supports both simple field-based searches and complex Airtable formula syntax for advanced filtering.

actions.name: Create or Update Record
actions.description: Intelligently updates an existing record if found, or creates a new record if none exists. Perfect for AI agents implementing upsert operations without manual record existence checks.

actions.name: Create Record
actions.description: Creates a new record in an Airtable table with specified field values. Perfect for AI agents to add structured data, populate databases, or create entries based on processed information.

actions.name: Create Records
actions.description: Creates multiple new records in batch (up to 10 records). Efficient for AI agents to bulk-insert structured data while maintaining referential integrity and reducing API calls.

actions.name: Update Record
actions.description: Updates specific fields in an existing Airtable record while preserving other field values. Perfect for AI agents to modify data based on processed information or user interactions.

actions.name: Delete Records
actions.description: Deletes multiple records in batch using their unique identifiers. Efficient for AI agents to clean up obsolete data or perform bulk maintenance operations.

actions.name: Delete Record
actions.description: Deletes a single record by its unique identifier. Useful for AI agents to remove specific data entries based on business logic or data lifecycle management.

actions.name: Get Latest Comments
actions.description: Retrieves up to 100 comments for a specific record, ordered from newest to oldest. Perfect for AI agents to analyze collaboration history and track record discussions.

actions.name: Create Comment
actions.description: Creates a new comment on a specific record for collaboration and documentation purposes. Ideal for AI agents to add contextual notes, status updates, or processing logs.

actions.name: Update Comment
actions.description: Updates the text content of an existing comment on a record. Note that users can only update comments they created. Perfect for AI agents to correct or enhance previously added notes.

actions.name: Delete Comment
actions.description: Deletes a comment from a record. Users can only delete comments they created (Enterprise Admins can delete any comment). Useful for AI agents to clean up outdated or incorrect notes.

idealUseCases: Automating data entry and record management across multiple Airtable bases
Building AI agents that need to sync and process structured data in real-time
Creating automated workflows that respond to new or updated records
Implementing data validation and cleanup processes for large datasets
Setting up content management systems with automated publishing workflows
Building inventory tracking and management systems
